question.content: For the ideal symmetric differential amplifier, estimate emitter quiescent current and dynamic metrics. Output exactly five comma-separated values in the order IEQ_mA,Ad,Ac,Ri_kOhm,Ro_kOhm; keep IEQ_mA to three digits after the decimal point and keep Ad, Ac, Ri_kOhm, Ro_kOhm to one digit after the decimal point; plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no percent sign, no fraction, no equation form, and no extra text.

step_1.content: Method: odd/even-mode decomposition with clamp-free small-signal equivalent blocks.
step_1.key_point: Extract IEQ first, then split odd-mode and even-mode paths for Ad and Ac.

step_3.content: Use DC bias branch to get IEQ and then map to gₘ and rπ for incremental analysis.
step_3.key_point: Parameter extraction is separated from gain derivation.

step_5.content: Compute Ad and Ri from the odd-mode half-circuit, then switch to even-mode for Ac and Ro.
step_5.key_point: Different excitations are solved in separate reduced circuits.

step_7.content: Assemble Ad, Ac, Ri, Ro in the required output order after rounding rules.
step_7.key_point: Keep IEQ to three decimals and remaining metrics to one decimal.

step_9.content: Output one comma-separated 5-value token list only.
step_9.key_point: No units and no extra text.
